other_observers: Nathan Pieplow, Andy Boyce; first spotted by Nathan
description: the bird was a very large gull, about twice as large as nearby Ring-billed Gulls. The overall shape was quite heavy compared to that of Ring-billed Gull, with a relatively thick neck, heavy chested look, short wings, and large bill all contributing to this. The bill was fairly large and very thick. The gonydeal angle was quite strong. The color of the bill was all yellow except for a red spot on the gonys, and a black spot between the gonys and bill tip on the lower mandible. The eye was pale, and the orbital ring was red. The legs were a pale pink color.  The head of the bird was a clean white, as were the underparts. The back was a very dark gray, almost black. The bird had a thin white tertial crescent. The wingtips were black, with medium sized white spots on the tips of the primaries. The tail was a clean white.Behaviors: The bird was loafing on the edge of the reservoir with other gulls, mostly Ring-billed Gulls.Call: none heardPlumage: adult basic
